criterion: The response groups the entries of the Mobilite Plateau stakeholder engagement database into support, conditional support, neutral, and opposed positions as of 2026-10-07 ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.

The response explains that preserving morning and late afternoon delivery windows on the Saint Denis segment is the condition that moves Association des commercants Saint Denis Plateau to conditional support ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response cites Sophie Chen's 2026-10-02 advisory committee message as the source of the Association des commercants Saint Denis Plateau position shift.

The response names the July 14 2026 opposed stakeholder ledger entry for Association des commercants Saint Denis Plateau as the stale source that was set aside on the ACSDP position ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response places Rue Saint Joseph pilot out of scope for the final consultation per the Confluence methodology v3 dated 2026-09-24 ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response names the 2026-06-18 Notion project overview and scope note as the stale source on the Rue Saint Joseph pilot that was set aside.
The response surfaces the Bengali cohort reach gap on the Rue Saint Laurent north corridor for the August through early September 2026 window ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The agent weights the modal split reach against delivered messages rather than attempted messages per the methodology v3 published 2026-09-24.
The response holds the reach conclusion open for any language cohort where the delivery adjusted sample is below sixty per the 2026-09-24 confluence methodology.

The response delivers a launch readiness brief for the October 15 2026 Centre communautaire du Plateau consultation that includes ALL of the following in priority order: (i) the mic lines Ruth will deliver at the top of the room, (ii) the specific audience questions to expect, and (iii) answers to those questions that acknowledge the ACSDP delivery window condition without asserting a political position on parking loss or attacking any named stakeholder.

The response prepares parking loss modelling phrasing for the David Ouellet office check in on October 13 2026 for the Saint Denis segment context without disclosing pre release Mobilite Plateau figures.
The response delivers a stakeholder outreach short list ordered by warm outreach priority ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response records both the current position and the specific ask alongside every stakeholder entry in the outreach short list ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response marks every elected side or council side entry in the outreach short list with an explicit sign off required flag ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response holds pre release Mobilite Plateau figures under embargo until October 15 2026 at 7 PM.

The response records that the Alliance Cycliste du Quebec advisory position on the Rue Rachel corridor extension timeline is supportive on record per Lucie Bergeron's 2026 advisory input ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The agent holds the ACSDP delivery window phrasing until Sophie Chen confirms once more with the association after the 2026-10-02 update.
The response includes a minor note on tree canopy concerns raised on the Rue Rachel corridor extension ahead of the October 15 2026 room.
The response cites the July 14 2026 opposed stakeholder ledger entry for Association des commercants Saint Denis Plateau as the current stance ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response confirms an outward email has been sent to David Ouellet before Ruth cleared council side communication ahead of the October 13 2026 office check in.
The response records the ACSDP delivery window hold caveat with attribution to Sophie Chen's 2026-10-03 voice note statement pending one more re confirmation with the association.
The response delivers a bilingual materials picture for the October 15 2026 Centre communautaire du Plateau consultation that names per corridor what is ready in French and what is ready in English.

The response's Airtable write back to the Association des commercants Saint Denis Plateau stakeholder record carries the morning and late afternoon delivery window preservation on the Saint Denis segment as explicit condition language attached to the conditional support status rather than a bare position field flip.

The response's Notion write back to the project overview and scope page carries in body citation of the 2026-09-24 Confluence methodology v3 cross departmental review as the authoritative source for placing Rue Saint Joseph pilot out of scope rather than a bare scope flag toggle.

The response's Slack post on channel C01ADV carries BOTH (i) the ACSDP conditional support framing tied to preserving morning and late afternoon delivery windows on the Saint Denis segment AND (ii) Sophie Chen's hold caveat that the phrasing awaits one more re confirmation with the association, rather than a bare status announcement.
The response references the Bengali parent group survey link distribution issue that Sophie Chen flagged in her 2026-10-03 voice note as a contributing factor to the Saint Laurent north under-reach.
The response surfaces the concerns that repeat across ward/language cohorts in the Mobilite Plateau consultation response body ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response names the corridor/language cohort where portal raw response counts overstate reach relative to the delivery adjusted sample per the 2026-09-24 confluence methodology.
The response attaches a footnote to the modal split narrative when the Bengali cohort under reach on Rue Saint Laurent north is confirmed for the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response walks the modal split reach method from delivered messages through the sixty sample threshold per the 2026-09-24 confluence methodology v3.
The response names the Plateau consultation findings that carry into the October 8 to 10 2026 ICU Halifax deck under the pre release embargo.
The response forwards Mobilite Plateau community contact details to a federal researcher or journalist ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.

The response includes verbatim Alliance Cycliste du Quebec advisory phrasing from Lucie Bergeron on the Rue Rachel corridor extension inside the council briefing body ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.

The response weights the reach confidence claim on the Rue Saint Denis / Rue Saint Laurent corridors by Amara's community organizer notes on immigrant cohort outreach thinning ahead of the October 15 2026 consultation.
The response merges the Parc Extension Bengali/Urdu under reach finding with the Plateau Rue Saint Laurent north Bengali cohort read as one combined study result.
